question.text: What is the definition of 'intermediaries' under the SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008?

answer.text: All entities registered with any financial regulator in India
Only stockbrokers and sub-brokers registered with stock exchanges
Any person dealing in securities whether registered with SEBI or not

Persons mentioned in sub-section 2 clauses (b) and (ba) of section 11 and sub-section (1) and (1A) of section 12 of the SEBI Act, including AMCs, clearing members, FPIs, and trading members, but excluding foreign venture capital investors, mutual funds, collective investment schemes, and venture capital funds

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The SEBI (Intermediaries) Regulations, 2008 specifically defines intermediaries with reference to sections 11 and 12 of SEBI Act, including AMCs, clearing members, FPIs, and trading members, while explicitly excluding foreign venture capital investors, mutual funds, collective investment schemes, and venture capital funds. **The Trap:** The broad definition might lead one to think it covers all financial entities, but the exclusions are specific and important. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series III-B and Series V-A also discuss intermediary definitions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding which entities are classified as intermediaries is crucial since the regulations impose specific obligations only on those covered.
